races.name: Aviari
races.description: Bird-like humanoids with wings, talons, and beaks. Known for aerial prowess and deep connection to the sky. Scouts and messengers who live in mountainous cliff communities.
races.flavor: "In the high peaks of Ryndor, where the wind never rests, the Aviari were born—neither bound to earth nor fully of the sky."
races.age: Reach adulthood around age 3; live up to 70 years.
races.alignment: Tend toward chaotic, varies by individual experience.
races.size: Medium (5–6 feet tall, 100–150 lbs, wingspan 8–10 ft).

speed.note: Cannot fly in medium or heavy armor, or while encumbered.

traits.name: Flightless
traits.description: Cannot use wings to fly until 5th level. At 5th level, gain the ability to fly for short periods. Must land at end of turn if flying (unless hovering).

traits.name: Keen Sight
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Mimicry
traits.description: You can mimic sounds you have heard, including voices, animal calls, and environmental sounds, after at least 1 minute of listening.

races.name: Cervar
races.description: Tall, slender, graceful humanoids with deer-like features: cloven hooves, large expressive eyes, pointed ears, and antlers (females). Natural healers and protectors of ancient forests.
races.flavor: "Cervar do not speak of the forest as something outside themselves. They are the forest—breathing, healing, remembering."
races.age: Mature at similar rate to humans; live up to 250 years.
races.alignment: Tend toward neutral or good; value balance and harmony.

traits.name: Healing Touch
traits.description: As an action, you can touch a creature and restore HP equal to your level + your Wisdom modifier. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). All uses are restored on a long rest.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Nature's Blessing
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Nature skill.

races.name: Drakarim
races.description: Rare, enigmatic humanoids with reptilian features: scales, tails, and sharp teeth. Skin ranges from deep red to metallic gold, with slit-pupiled glowing eyes. Strong connection to dragonkind.
races.flavor: "The Drakarim do not merely carry dragon blood—they carry dragon memory. Every scale is a scar from the Age of Fire."
races.age: Mature at the same rate as humans; live up to 120 years.
races.alignment: Strong-willed and individualistic; varies widely by upbringing.

traits.name: Draconic Ancestry
traits.description: Choose one dragon type from the Draconic Ancestry table (PHB). You gain a breath weapon and damage resistance associated with that ancestry.

traits.name: Draconic Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to the damage type associated with your draconic ancestry.

traits.name: Breath Weapon
traits.description: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. The size, shape, and damage type are determined by your draconic ancestry. Recharges on a short or long rest.

traits.name: Tail Swipe
traits.description: You can make a melee attack with your tail (5 ft. reach), dealing 1d6 + Strength modifier bludgeoning damage.

races.name: Khuzud
races.description: Ancient reclusive dwarves who refused to surface. Completely blind with no eyes, translucent skin, and no vocal cords. Communicate via Inglishmek—an echolocative sign language using tongue clicks and gestures.
races.flavor: "The Khuzud have lived in darkness so long they no longer see it as darkness. To them, the world resonates. Every footstep, every heartbeat—a map of everything."

ability_scores.note: No standard ASI listed—work with your GM for Constitution or Intelligence bonus.

races.age: Live up to 400 years. Considered young until age 60.
races.alignment: Tend toward neutrality; focus on invention, discovery, and advancement.
languages: Inglishmek (echolocative sign language only — cannot speak any language)

traits.name: Blind (Echolocation)
traits.description: You have no eyes but rely on echolocation. You can perceive your surroundings within 60 feet without sight. You have superior hearing and are effective in combat through echolocation.

traits.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
traits.description: You prefer dark underground places. You suffer harm when exposed to sunlight for extended periods. In direct sunlight, you have disadvantage on attack rolls and Perception checks relying on sight.

traits.name: Ancient Ancestry (Underground Favored Terrain)
traits.description: Underground is always your favored terrain. You gain double proficiency bonus on proficient skill checks related to underground environments. While traveling for 1+ hour underground: difficult terrain doesn't slow your group, you can't become lost, you can move stealthily at normal pace when alone, you find twice as much food when foraging, and you learn exact details about creatures you track.

traits.name: Inglishmek
traits.description: You cannot speak any language—you have no vocal cords. You communicate exclusively through Inglishmek, a complex sign and echolocative language. You can read and write Common but cannot vocalize.

races.name: Leoporin
races.description: Rabbit-like humanoids; small, lean, and incredibly fast. Covered in soft velvety fur in earthy tones, with large expressive eyes and powerful hind legs. Skilled hunters, gatherers, and burrowers who live in tight-knit forest communities.
races.flavor: "A Leoporin at rest is a Leoporin planning their next sprint. Still waters, they say—but the current beneath never stops."
races.age: Reach maturity at age 15; live up to 60 years.
races.alignment: Tend toward neutrality; can be of any alignment.

traits.name: Swift Feet
traits.description: When you take the Dash action, your speed is tripled instead of doubled.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Burst of Speed
traits.description: As a bonus action, you can move an additional 10 feet. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1). All uses are restored on a long rest.

races.name: Nebernorian
races.description: Mysterious beings from beyond the Rift, hailing from the plane of Nebernoria. Humanoid in appearance with elongated limbs and large kaleidoscope eyes that shift through impossible colors.
races.flavor: "When a Nebernorian looks at you, you get the sense that they're seeing not just you—but every version of you that ever could have been."

ability_scores.note: Work with your GM — this race is still being developed for the Ryndor sourcebook.

races.age: Unknown — their relationship to time may differ fundamentally from other races.
languages: work with GM for additional languages

traits.name: Rift-Born Senses
traits.description: Your eyes, kaleidoscope in nature, perceive more than the material plane. Work with your GM to define the specific traits of your Nebernorian character.

traits.name: Under Development
traits.description: The Nebernorian race is introduced in the sourcebook but lacks full mechanical details. All traits should be established in collaboration with your Game Master.

races.name: Human
races.description: The most widespread and adaptable people of Ryndor. Humans thrive everywhere—from city spires to frontier wilds—driven by ambition, curiosity, and an outsized impact on the world.
races.flavor: "Humans are brief candles that burn so bright the world remembers their light long after they are gone."
races.age: Reach adulthood in late teens; rarely live more than a century.
races.alignment: No strong tendency — humans run the full gamut.
races.size: Medium (5–6 feet tall, widely varying build).

traits.name: Adaptable
traits.description: You gain proficiency in one additional skill of your choice, reflecting the breadth of human experience.

traits.name: Extra Language
traits.description: You can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

traits.name: Determined
traits.description: When you fail an ability check by 1–4, you may reroll once per short rest. You must use the new result.

races.name: Elf
races.description: Ancient and graceful, elves are long-lived people deeply in tune with magic and nature. In Ryndor, elven communities are keepers of forgotten lore and guardians of primordial places.
races.flavor: "To know an elf is to know a living memory—they have walked paths that your great-grandparents never dreamed of."
races.age: Reach adulthood at 100; can live to 750 years.
races.alignment: Lean toward freedom and self-expression, often neutral or chaotic good.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Keen Senses
traits.description: You have proficiency in the Perception skill.

traits.name: Fey Ancestry
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put you to sleep.

traits.name: Trance
traits.description: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply for 4 hours a day (the elven trance), gaining the same benefit a human gets from 8 hours of sleep.

traits.name: Elf Weapon Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with the longsword, shortsword, shortbow, and longbow.

races.name: Tiefling
races.description: Bearing the bloodline of an ancient infernal pact, tieflings carry the weight of a legacy they did not choose. In Ryndor, they are often wanderers and scholars, proving their worth against the world's suspicion.
races.flavor: "We did not ask for these horns. But we have learned to make the world answer for its stares."
races.age: Mature at the same rate as humans; live slightly longer, up to 120 years.
races.alignment: No intrinsic alignment, though their outsider status often pushes them toward chaos.
races.size: Medium (5–6 feet tall, similar build to humans).

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Thanks to your infernal heritage, you have superior vision in dark conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light.

traits.name: Hellish Resistance
traits.description: You have resistance to fire damage.

traits.name: Infernal Legacy
traits.description: You know the Thaumaturgy cantrip. At 3rd level, you can cast Hellish Rebuke as a 2nd-level spell once per long rest. At 5th level, you can cast Darkness once per long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these.

races.name: Dwarf
races.description: Stout, resilient, and fiercely proud, dwarves are a people forged in stone and tempered by centuries of tradition. In Ryndor, dwarven clans are master craftsmen, warriors, and keepers of mountain strongholds.
races.flavor: "A dwarf does not bend. A dwarf endures. The mountain taught us that—and we have been teaching it to our enemies ever since."
races.age: Considered young until age 50; live up to 350 years.
races.alignment: Tend toward law and tradition, most often lawful good.
races.size: Medium (4–5 feet tall, 150 lbs and above).

speed.note: Speed is not reduced by wearing heavy armor.

traits.name: Darkvision
traits.description: Accustomed to life underground, you can see in dim light within 60 feet as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

traits.name: Dwarven Resilience
traits.description: You have advantage on saving throws against poison, and you have resistance against poison damage.

traits.name: Dwarven Combat Training
traits.description: You have proficiency with the battleaxe, handaxe, light hammer, and warhammer.

traits.name: Tool Proficiency
traits.description: You gain proficiency with one type of artisan's tools of your choice: smith's tools, brewer's supplies, or mason's tools.

traits.name: Stonecunning
traits.description: Whenever you make an Intelligence (History) check related to the origin of stonework, you are considered proficient in the History skill and add double your proficiency bonus to the check.
